questions.question: A customer receives a product with incomplete safety information and later argues that the seller should have disclosed proper usage conditions. Which right is most directly involved, and why?
options: Right to be informed, because the issue concerns access to accurate product information before use.
Right to safety, because every complaint about product use is handled only under protection from hazards.
Right to choose, because incomplete information mainly reduces the number of brands available.
Right to be heard, because the buyer has already raised a complaint after purchase.

questions.question: Statement I: Consumer protection benefits consumers by giving them legal safeguards. Statement II: It also benefits businesses because fair practices can build trust. Which option best reflects the NCERT position?
options: Both statements are valid, and Statement II prevents treating consumer protection as only a consumer-side issue.
Both statements are valid, but Statement II is unrelated because businesses gain only through advertising.
Statement I is valid, while Statement II is invalid because consumer protection only creates legal pressure.
Statement I is invalid, while Statement II is valid because law protects business reputation first.
